implementations.title: Minimum domain then maximum degree for UVERTEX
implementations.reason: Selecting vertices with smallest domain (most constrained) and among those highest degree (most constraining) focuses search on hard subproblems, reducing branching.

implementations.title: Max domain then min available colors for CVERTEX
implementations.reason: Uncoloring vertices with large domain but few available colors (bad count) helps to break deadlocks more effectively, as they are more constrained.

implementations.title: Impact-based color selection
implementations.reason: Choosing a color that minimizes the number of neighbors that become singleton or have reduced domain reduces future conflicts and dead ends.

implementations.title: Simple greedy by degree
implementations.reason: A straightforward greedy that colors vertices in decreasing degree order, using the smallest available color. This is fast and often provides a good initial solution.
